assignments.description: Set most things to blank because flows don't like null
assignments.name: Assign_Batch_Values
assignments.label: Assign Batch Values

Flow.description: Portal Screen Flow that initiates the batch creation process
Flow.label: ABS FORM - Create Batch

recordLookups.name: Get_Grant_Recipient_Lead_for_Award
recordLookups.label: Get Grant Recipient Lead for Award

fields.name: NewBatchCreated
fields.fieldText:
Batch Initiation Complete

Local Authority Batch Reference: {!BatchNew.LA_Identifier__c}

Version Number: {!BatchNew.Version_Number__c}

fields.name: ErrorHeader
fields.fieldText: No Award Baseline was found for this funding award, please inform your PSO

fields.name: memberDetail
fields.fieldText: Person responsible for Batch Creation

fields.name: GRLDetail
fields.fieldText: Grant Recipient responsible for Batch Submission

fields.name: SchemeDeclaration
fields.fieldText:
1A. The lead Grant Recipient (GR) affirms that all houses submitted as part of this batch have received retrofit assessments and a medium-term improvement plan from a retrofit coordinator. The GR will also ensure that a PAS2035 Project is created by the retrofit team in the Trustmark Data Warehouse for all homes approved as part of this batch within 4 weeks of the batch approval data, and final lodgements are completed within 6 weeks of the works being completed for the household.

All upgrades must be compliant with the latest PAS 2035 guidance. Failure to comply with PAS 2035 standards may lead to clawback of payment. Under PAS 2035 standards all homes and measures must be logged appropriately in the TrustMark Data Warehouse, within a specific timeframe. Installers must be Trustmark registered and certified to the latest version of the relevant standards as specified in the MoU and published policy guidance. GRs are responsible for ensuring compliance with the latest version of the quality and installation standards.

 

1B. The lead GR affirms that all houses submitted as part of this batch have consented to receive measures.

All households must consent to receiving measures under WH:LG. The ultimate decision to install the package of measures selected and designed by the Retrofit Coordinator rests solely with the household. Note, if the property is partially owned by others or others are involved in the process (e.g. landlords, tenants, freeholders, trustees or equity release companies) LAs must obtain evidence of their permission.

 

1C. If this batch includes private rented (PRS) homes, the lead GR confirms that the landlord/s for each PRS home have completed the required PRS Form and the lead GR has received confirmation from the delivery partner on whether the landlord/s of each home is eligible for 100% funding, or 50% funding only.

The PRS form is designed to obtain the information required for Private Rented Sector households to be upgraded under the WH: LG. This includes the details which will be used to check whether the landlord’s home should be fully funded by the grant or 50% funded by the landlord.

 

1D. The lead GR affirms that the batch meets the scheme's desired EPC aspirations to improve homes to EPC band C where possible, and/or where this target is not possible takes a value for money and strategic approach. The lead GR confirms that homes already treated under a previous LAD or HUG phase will meet the strict requirements of being improved to EPC band C and/or receiving low carbon heat as a condition of receiving WH:LG funding.

All homes submitted as part of WH:LG should aspire to reach EPC C and this should be considered when developing retrofit assessments. This not a requirement, unless the home received upgrades through the LAD or HUG schemes. Homes that received upgrades through LAD or HUG will be required to be improved to EPC band C and/or receiving low carbon heat as a condition of receiving WH:LG funding. For homes that did not received upgrades through the LAD and HUG schemes, if it is not possible to reach EPC C the GR must be able to evidence that a value for money and strategic approach has been taken.

 

1E. The lead GR confirms that works will not be undertaken on any of the properties connected to this batch application, until it has received explicit confirmation from DESNZ that this batch has been approved and works can commence. Note, pre-approval does not count as approval to proceed with upgrades.

Advanced funding has been provided to GRs to enable the continuous delivery of upgrades, however this must not be used to upgrade homes until DESNZ has approved the given batch. Note, that pre-approval applies to GRs that have not yet passed DAC but want to pre-approve upgrades for their pipeline of homes. This status will be updated to approved once the GR passes DAC, and only then can work begin to upgrade those homes.

 

1F. The lead GR confirms that all costs included within this batch are accurate quotes from the relevant delivery partners/installers.

At the point of submitting a batch application, the measures and works proposed should all be fully costed, with quotes obtained and agreed with installers to help to minimise the number of batch edits required and potential changes to the payment amount to be made to the GR.

 

1G. The lead GR is responsible for meeting the WH:LG policy requirements- e.g. cost caps, measure price limits and social housing caps - across the project (including all consortia members) by project completion.

The lead GR will need to monitor the project performance against policy requirements such as the cost caps and measure price limits must ensure it does not breach the permitted thresholds, on average across all homes treated, by project closure.

 

1H. The lead GR acknowledges that they must adhere to the MoU agreement, including requirements from 129 to 144 specifically covering the batch system and batch payments.

fields.name: RedConsortiumHeader
fields.fieldText: You do not have permission to create batches.

value.stringValue: Assign LAs,Batch Delivery Completion,Property Information,Measures,Capital Expenditure,VAT,Summary,Validation
